results.title: Hallucination Detection via Topological Divergence on Attention Graphs (2025/4 v3)
results.snippet: LLM-side: topological divergence on attention graphs identifies hallucination-aware heads.
results.func_justification: Uses topology-derived features over attention graphs as an audit/anomaly signal for hallucination detection. Operationally implements the same effect cluster as candidate: topology-of-attention as structural audit signal, despite different topological invariants (persistent homology / graph divergence vs. Thom-Berry catastrophe class labels). Functional equivalence above 0.7 threshold.

results.title: Gradient-Based Model Fingerprinting (2025/6)
results.snippet: LLM-side: gradient-response fingerprinting for LLM family classification.
results.func_justification: Fingerprints models from gradient responses for family identification, not structural classification of attention-spike geometry. Different effect cluster: model-attribution vs. spike-structure audit.

results.title: SentinelAgent: Graph-based MAS Anomaly Detection (2025/5)
results.snippet: LLM-side: dynamic graph anomaly detection in MAS.
results.func_justification: Multi-agent-system interaction-graph anomaly detection at inter-agent level, not within-model attention-spike geometry. Different granularity and substrate.

results.title: LLM-Based Anomaly + OOD Detection (NAACL 2025)
results.snippet: LLM-side: taxonomy of LLM anomaly/OOD detection methods.
results.func_justification: Broad survey/taxonomy of anomaly and OOD detection methods using LLMs; no catastrophe-class or attention-spike topology mechanism.

results.title: AXIS: Explainable Time-Series Anomaly Detection (2025/9)
results.snippet: LLM-side: explainable AD; uses structure-aware features.
results.func_justification: Time-series anomaly detection on external signals using LLM-based explainability; not attention-spike topology audit inside a transformer.

matched_effect_clusters: topology-based attention audit (Hallucination Detection via Topological Divergence on Attention Graphs)
notes: Independent re-judgment confirms rank 4 (Topological Divergence on Attention Graphs) as a functional hit under the FROZEN OR rule (func 0.74 >= 0.7; also kw>=2 via attention+topology). All 7 other results below all three thresholds. total_hits=1, so verdict FAIL.
